meta.source: DeFi Llama, Rekt News, SlowMist, CertiK, Chainalysis, BlockSec Phalcon

incidents.description: Attackers posed as a quant firm for 3 weeks, socially engineering Drift's Security Council into pre-signing durable nonce transactions. Fake CVT token whitelisted as collateral, vaults drained in 12 minutes.
incidents.lesson: Durable nonce pre-signing is a fatal attack surface. Never pre-sign admin transactions for unverified counterparties.

derived_source_evidence.rights_envelope: Public API metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw dumps.

incidents.description: Public incident data classifies the Silo V2 loss as an Arbitrum lending-market oracle misconfiguration / protocol-logic exploit. Exact vulnerable line remains source-review-only until a protocol postmortem is promoted.
incidents.lesson: Oracle configs must be validated in production. Use multiple independent price sources.

derived_source_evidence.rights_envelope: Public incident writeup metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw article text.

incidents.description: Attacker manipulated protocol reserves to mint unbacked tokens and dump them on open markets.
incidents.lesson: Reserve ratios must be continuously monitored and bounded by on-chain sanity checks.

derived_source_evidence.rights_envelope: Public API metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw dumps.

incidents.description: BlockSec reports that a post-audit LP-accounting refactor produced a slightly negative intermediate value that was cast from int256 to uint256 without a boundary check, inflating realized PnL during a single-sided LP and long-trade sequence.
incidents.lesson: Transparency in post-incident disclosure is essential for community trust and collective defense.

derived_source_evidence.rights_envelope: Public incident writeup metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw article text.

incidents.description: Malicious actor targeted ETH cross-chain bridge contract controlling GPU-cloud collateral. Team isolated the contract rapidly.
incidents.lesson: Bridge contracts need circuit breakers and real-time anomaly detection for rapid response.

derived_source_evidence.rights_envelope: Public API metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw dumps.

incidents.description: Attacker forged cross-chain messages via a Merkle Mountain Range (MMR) proof replay, minting 1B bridged DOT tokens and dumping them.
incidents.lesson: Bridge verification must reject replayed state proofs and enforce strict nonce uniqueness.

derived_source_evidence.rights_envelope: Public API metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw dumps.

incidents.description: Broken permission model allowed unauthorized withdrawal from a treasury contract.
incidents.lesson: Treasury contracts should require multi-sig and timelocks for any outbound transfers.

derived_source_evidence.rights_envelope: Public API metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw dumps.

incidents.description: Bridge aggregator allowed 'donation' of negative amounts, crediting the attacker with tokens they never deposited.
incidents.lesson: Never trust unsigned input amounts. Validate donation parameters with strict lower bounds.

derived_source_evidence.rights_envelope: Public API metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw dumps.

incidents.description: BurnAddress accounting bug allowed attacker to manipulate token balances and extract excess value.
incidents.lesson: Accounting around burn/mint addresses must be audited as a first-class invariant.

derived_source_evidence.rights_envelope: Public API metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw dumps.

incidents.description: Hot wallet social-engineering incident led to unauthorized outbound transfers.
incidents.lesson: Hot wallets should be rate-limited and require dual approval for large transfers.

derived_source_evidence.rights_envelope: Public API metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw dumps.

incidents.description: Attacker prepared 423 wallets and 8 fake liquidity pools 2 days in advance to manipulate oracles, then used flash loans to extract $18.4M.
incidents.lesson: Oracle manipulation requires deep liquidity monitoring. Fake pool detection should be part of oracle risk models.

derived_source_evidence.rights_envelope: Public API metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw dumps.

incidents.description: 54 hot wallets drained in a coordinated attack. Platform suspended operations immediately.
incidents.lesson: Exchange operational security must include HSMs, withdrawal limits, and geographic signing distribution.

derived_source_evidence.rights_envelope: Public API metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw dumps.

incidents.description: Attacker drained 116,500 rsETH via LayerZero OFT bridge with a 1-of-1 DVN configuration. Stolen rsETH deposited into Aave as collateral, borrowing $236M in real WETH.
incidents.lesson: Single-DVN bridge configs are catastrophically fragile. Require >=2 independent verifiers and monitor DVN health.

derived_source_evidence.rights_envelope: Public API metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw dumps.

incidents.description: REVLoans accepted a caller-supplied loan accounting source without verifying it was protocol-registered; a forged high-decimal same-currency context polluted totalBorrowedFrom and enabled borrowing at an inflated share price.
incidents.lesson: Even small treasuries benefit from multi-sig, timelocks, and on-chain spending limits.

incidents.attack_vector: first depositor / zero-supply vault share inflation
incidents.description: SlowMist classifies the incident as a first-depositor attack against a newly deployed vault: minimal initial shares plus direct asset transfer distorted the asset-to-share ratio before later user deposits.
incidents.lesson: Options and structured products need continuous Greeks monitoring and circuit breakers.

derived_source_evidence.rights_envelope: Public incident writeup metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw article text.
Public API metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw dumps.

incidents.description: Admin key leak led to unauthorized contract upgrades and fund extraction.
incidents.lesson: Admin keys must be stored in HSMs or hardware wallets. Single-EOA admin is indefensible.

derived_source_evidence.rights_envelope: Public API metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw dumps.

incidents.description: BlockSec classifies the Kipseli Router incident as improper validation; its blog summary identifies the detector-relevant pattern as a router quote or decimal-mismatch risk.
incidents.lesson: Low-TVL protocols are not immune; attackers often use them as testing grounds.

derived_source_evidence.rights_envelope: Public incident writeup metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw article text.
Public API metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw dumps.

incidents.description: EIP-712 signature replay combined with unlimited ERC-20 approval drained user wallets.
incidents.lesson: Signatures must be strictly non-replayable with chainId, contract address, and nonce binding.

derived_source_evidence.rights_envelope: Public API metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw dumps.

incidents.description: BlockSec classifies Purrlend as a key-compromise incident. For detector purposes, 0guard treats it as privileged-role takeover and unbacked bridge-token mint risk, not as a source-confirmed calldata exploit.
incidents.lesson: Solana programs need the same rigor as EVM contracts: formal verification, audits, and fuzzing.

incidents.description: BlockSec classifies Scallop as an accounting-flaw incident and summarizes the detector pattern as cross-pool index injection, which 0guard maps to account-binding and rewards-accounting invariants.
incidents.lesson: Rapid incident response and contract pausing can limit losses significantly.

incidents.attack_vector: oracle misconfiguration / invalid pool fee tier
incidents.description: BlockSec reports that a Base vault used an invalid Uniswap V3 fee tier, causing pricing to resolve to a non-existent pool and value non-USDC reserves at zero before share minting and redemption.
incidents.lesson: Cross-chain yield strategies multiply attack surface — each bridge is a potential failure point.

derived_source_evidence.rights_envelope: Public incident writeup metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw article text.
Public API metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw dumps.

incidents.description: GatewayEVM smart contract exploit forced a halt of all cross-chain activity while the team patched.
incidents.lesson: Gateway contracts should be pausable with a security council that can act in minutes, not hours.

derived_source_evidence.rights_envelope: Public API metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw dumps.

incidents.description: BlockSec lists the JUDAO Token incident as an access-control issue. Public detail is still high-level, so 0guard maps this to generic privileged-call and upgrade-path review.
incidents.lesson: DAO treasuries should require on-chain voting + timelock for any non-routine outbound transfers.

incidents.description: Over-the-counter settlement protocol incident. Limited public details.
incidents.lesson: OTC settlement needs atomic swaps or escrow — never trust-based finality.

derived_source_evidence.rights_envelope: Public API metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw dumps.

incidents.description: Signedness mismatch in perpetuals settlement math allowed attacker to extract excess collateral.
incidents.lesson: Numeric type safety (signed vs unsigned) is critical in financial contracts. Use checked math.

derived_source_evidence.rights_envelope: Public API metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw dumps.

incidents.description: Refund logic drain exploited a flaw in reward distribution, allowing repeated claims.
incidents.lesson: Refund and reward loops must be strictly idempotent and bounded by claim windows.

derived_source_evidence.rights_envelope: Public API metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw dumps.

incidents.attack_vector: commons bridge compromise / privileged upgrade flaw
incidents.description: Public reports link the loss to the Commons bridge on Base and describe a privileged upgrade or bridge-control flaw used to drain SYND before swapping to ETH. Treat as bridge/privileged-control risk until a protocol postmortem is published.
incidents.lesson: Investment pools should enforce deposit caps and withdrawal cooldowns to limit exploit blast radius.

derived_source_evidence.rights_envelope: Public news metadata. Use as corroborating bridge-risk evidence; do not mirror article text.
Public API metadata. Use as source-cited defensive evidence; do not mirror raw dumps.

incidents.description: Single EOA admin key (wasabideployer.eth) compromised across Ethereum, Base, Berachain, and Blast. Attacker granted admin role, upgraded UUPS proxies, and drained vaults in ~2 hours.
incidents.lesson: Single-EOA admin keys are indefensible. Multisig + timelock on all admin and upgrade functions is the baseline.
